summary: A superb matrix capture of the legendary 5/8/77 Cornell show, with crystal‑clear sound and a fire‑filled set highlighted by Scarlet Fire, Row Jimmy and St. Stephen.
review: The Hunter Seamons matrix blends pristine soundboard clarity with intimate audience ambience, delivering an almost “plugged‑in” experience of the famed Cornell concert.  The performance is repeatedly described as one of the band’s peak moments – blistering jams on Not Fade Away, a soaring Row Jimmy, a searing Scarlet Fire and a transcendent St. Stephen sandwich that left fans calling it “the best ever.”  Across more than thirty reviews the consensus is overwhelmingly positive.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 detailed reviews, 23 five‑star and only three four‑star ratings; reviewers praise both the musical performance (standout jams, energy) and the technical matrix mix (crisp, balanced, crowd present).  This yields a top‑tier rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp soundboard core with intimate audience noise; Phil’s bass and keyboards are exceptionally clear, Jerry’s guitar tone described as “top form” and “crisp”. No noticeable low‑end dominance or drop‑outs; reviewers note perfect blend of two worlds.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set launches with high energy (Minglewood, Brown Eyed Woman, Row Jimmy, Jack Straw) and builds into a fiery second set where the St. Stephen‑NFA sandwich, Scarlet Fire and Morning Dew create an unforgettable arc.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for being in top form, crisp tone, straight‑ahead lead work and driving the show’s energy.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass described as “kickin’”, crystal clear, the engine of the performance; several reviewers highlight his low‑end presence.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals noted as strong (e.g., tearing into lyrics on Supplication); rhythm guitar supportive throughout.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards praised for clarity and presence in the mix; no complaints about mud or loss of detail.
